title: Truffle Oil Comforting Brazilian Garlicky Fried Rice
description: A comforting Brazilian dish featuring truffle oil, garlicky flavors, and a glassy-jelly texture, making it a great Jain-friendly option for a satisfying meal.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onions and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the cooked rice to the skillet, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Pour in the truffle oil and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes, until the rice is well coated.
Create four holes in the rice mixture, then crack an egg into each hole.
Use the spatula to scramble the eggs and mix them into the rice, then stir-fry for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the chopped cilantro and salt to the rice mixture, and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Serve the fried rice hot with additional truffle oil to taste, if desired.
Garnish with a sprinkle of cilantro and a drizzle of truffle oil, if desired.
